fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Louisiana Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Louisiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity louisiana, how to transfer audiologist license to louisiana, audiologist compact louisiana, louisiana audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Louisiana Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.physical_address: 18550 Highland Road, Suite B, Baton Rouge, LA 70809

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Louisiana.

steps.title: Register for a New Applicant Account
steps.description: Create a new user account on the LBESPA Application Portal at the provided URL. You will need to click 'First Time User? Click Here to Register' to begin.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the LBESPA Application Portal using your new account credentials and complete the Audiology Initial Licensure Application form, ensuring all required information is provided.

steps.title: Pay Application and Licensure Fees
steps.description: Submit the required initial application fee ($150) and the initial licensure fee ($150) directly through the LBESPA Application Portal.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official academic transcripts, proof of supervised professional practice (e.g., Supervisor's Report), and any other required documentation to be sent directly to the Louisiana Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology (LBESPA).

steps.title: Pass National Examination
steps.description: Ensure that official scores from the national Praxis examination in Audiology (test code 5245) are submitted directly to LBESPA from the testing agency.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: Monitor the status of your application through the LBESPA Application Portal and respond to any requests for additional information from the Board. Once approved, your license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Louisiana audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Louisiana have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Louisiana participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Louisiana without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Louisiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Louisiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Louisiana
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Louisiana, you must use the LBESPA Application Portal to submit your online application and supporting documents.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the LBESPA Application Portal, click 'First Time User? Click Here to Register' to create a new applicant account, then log in to begin the Audiology licensure application.
